# Links, Images & Sanitizer

## Links

Here is an [inline link](https://example.com) in a paragraph.

Multiple links: [GitHub](https://github.com), [Google](https://google.com), and [mailto](mailto:user@example.com).

A link with **[bold text](https://example.com/bold)** and *[italic text](https://example.com/italic)*.

Link with `code` inside: [`npm install`](https://npmjs.com).

Bare URL (autolink): <https://example.com/autolink>

## Images

An image: ![Alt text for photo](https://example.com/photo.png)

Image with no alt: ![](https://example.com/no-alt.png)

Image inside a paragraph with text before ![icon](https://example.com/icon.svg) and after.

## Thematic Breaks

Content above the break.

---

Content between breaks.

***

Content after the last break.

## Mixed Inline Formatting with Links

This paragraph has **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, `inline code`, and a [link](https://example.com) all together.

> A blockquote with a [link inside](https://example.com/quoted).

- List item with [a link](https://example.com/list)
- Another item with ![an image](https://example.com/list-img.png)

## Table with Links

| Name | URL |
|------|-----|
| Example | [example.com](https://example.com) |
| GitHub | [github.com](https://github.com) |

## Edge Cases

Link with empty href: [empty]()

Link with special characters: [query params](https://example.com/search?q=hello+world&lang=en)

Image with long alt text: ![This is a very long alt text that describes the image in great detail for accessibility purposes](https://example.com/long-alt.png)
